hazard.summary: HTTP-client retries on 429 occurred below the application layer, preventing the CEL program from seeing the rate-limit signal it was designed to handle. Hidden retries consumed additional budget and amplified pressure until the upstream window reset.
signals: Greenhouse pagination limit is 3 requests per 30 seconds
429 responses include no Retry-After or reset headers
underlying HTTP client automatically retries with exponential backoff
CEL program's explicit 429 handler never executes
observed sequence of rapid 200s followed by 429 retry cascade
retried requests eventually succeed once the rate-limit window resets

constraints: Application-layer rate handling must not be bypassed by hidden transport retries.
When 429 responses provide no reset semantics, proactive pacing is safer than reactive retry.
Retry ownership must live at the layer that has the necessary budget and pagination context.
Successful-response budget headers should be used to stop pagination before entering a 429 cascade.
verification: Verify the CEL program can observe and respond to rate-limit conditions instead of having them consumed by the HTTP client.
Verify proactive pacing keeps /events requests within the 3/30s pagination limit.
Verify pagination stops when X-RateLimit-Remaining budget is exhausted on successful responses.
Verify 429 retry cascades no longer occur under pagination load.
notes: This receipt is strong because the problem is not simply missing backoff. The deeper failure is retry ownership at the wrong layer: the transport retried automatically, while the application layer — which had pagination and budget context — lost the ability to control behavior. The chosen mitigation is proactive pacing plus successful-response budget checks rather than reactive 429 recovery.

constraints: keep retry ownership at the layer with rate context
use proactive pacing when reset semantics are absent
avoid hidden transport retries that mask application control
anti_patterns: pagination continues after remaining budget reaches zero
